mappings.rationale: Directly covers molecular structure, chemical bonding, organic and inorganic compounds, and the classification of chemical substances central to this section.
Directly covers molecular structure, shapes, bonds, and the relationship between molecular structure and the properties of bulk matter.
Covers chemical bonding theory, molecular spectra, quantum mechanical treatments of bonding, and thermodynamic methods for molecular characterization.
Directly covers all major groups of organic compounds discussed in the section including hydrocarbons, alcohols, carboxylic acids, amino acids, and polymers.
Directly relevant to X-ray, electron, and neutron diffraction methods for determining molecular structures discussed in this section.
Relevant to understanding how elemental properties and electron configurations determine chemical bonding and molecular structure.
Covers the quantum mechanical framework underlying molecular orbital theory and the quantum-mechanical treatment of chemical bonding in this section.
Relevant to the section's coverage of nucleic acids (DNA and RNA), proteins, amino acids, and the molecular basis of biological macromolecules.
Covers the molecular structures of biological molecules including proteins, nucleic acids, carbohydrates, and lipids discussed in this section.
Covers the historical development of molecular structure theory and chemical bonding concepts from early ideas to quantum mechanics.
Relevant to magnetic resonance spectrometry and magnetic susceptibility methods used for determining molecular structures discussed in this section.
Relevant to spectrochemical methods including infrared, ultraviolet, and Raman spectroscopy used to identify molecular structures.
Relevant to the spatial arrangement of atoms, molecular symmetry, stereoisomers, and conformational analysis discussed in this section.
Relevant to the elemental composition of compounds and the classification of inorganic compounds discussed in this section.
Covers molecular-scale imaging and characterization techniques relevant to the experimental procedures for determining molecular structures.
Relevant to the instrumental methods and diffraction techniques used for determining molecular structures discussed in this section.
Relevant to the relationship between molecular structure and properties of bulk matter, including polymers and crystalline compounds.
Relevant to the section's coverage of proteins, amino acids, and the molecular structure of biological macromolecules.
Relevant to radiochemical methods including radiometric analysis, activation analysis, and isotopic dilution used in molecular analysis.
Covers organic polymers, molecular interactions, and the relationship between molecular structure and macroscopic material properties.
Relevant to understanding bonding in solids, metallic bonds, and the electronic structure of molecules and materials.
Relevant to the molecular structure of nucleic acids (DNA and RNA), nucleotides, and nucleosides discussed in this section.
Relevant to the molecular structure and characterization of nucleic acids, a key class of organic compounds in this section.
Covers the nature of chemical bonding, molecular bonds, and how molecular structure relates to the physical states of matter.
Relevant to the molecular structure of steroids, peptides, and organic compounds that function as hormones discussed in this section.
